observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to record collections in the Report of
Collections and Deposits in the same month they were actually received;
2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to properly
record the unreported collections of ₱31,491.93 in CY 2024.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to perform periodic reconciliation of tax withholdings, remittances, and the Due
to BIR account to promptly identify and correct discrepancies, and to prepare the
necessary adjusting journal entry to fairly present the account in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure prompt and full remittance of all taxes withheld to prevent under/over
remittance and to avoid penalties and surcharges. We further recommended that
reconciliation be made between the amounts withheld and its remittances during
the current and past years and make the needed adjustments if necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure the timely remittance of the insurance premium contributions and loan
amortizations to prevent the risk of possible loss or misuse and avoid penalties and
surcharges on the municipality and responsible officials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Ensure the timely enrollment of all employees to the Philhealth system and to
prevent future non-remittance issues.
2. Promptly remit the unremitted ₱28,456.54 in Philhealth contributions to
PHIC to comply with regulations and avoid penalties and surcharges.
3. Strictly implement monitoring and reconciliation procedures to ensure that
all deducted contributions are properly recorded and remitted on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and
the contractor to execute Certificates of Turnover and Acceptance documents for
each project between the contractor and the Municipality, as well as between the
Municipality and the respective barangays. Additionally, we recommend that
these certificates be attached as supporting documents when derecognizing the
projects from the Municipality’s books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
strictly comply with Item 1.2.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 by requiring an
authorization letter and valid identification documents before releasing payments
to job order personnel when claimed by someone other than the payee.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRM Office to
optimally utilize the LDRRM Fund through the full implementation of the
LDRRM projects. Likewise, we recommended directing the MDRRM Office to
clearly specify the programs, projects and activities in the Annual Budget, AIP
and LDRRMFIP to ensure proper implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to continue ensuring the full implementation of GAD PPAs
and the optimal utilization of the GAD Fund in the coming years. This will further
enhance the municipality’s efforts in addressing gender-related concerns
effectively and efficiently.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:

1.	Formulate 20% DF projects into well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready as considered by the Municipal Development Council before their inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and Annual Budget; and

2.	Conduct regular monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of the 20% DF projects, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned in accordance with the approved development plan.

observations.reason: See related discussion in finding no. 4, Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

1.	LDRRM Officer to review the PPAs included in the LDRRM Investment Plan and ensure that the PPAs address the mandated four (4) thematic areas, and strictly utilize and/or implement them with the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact as required under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013;
	
2.	Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund as required under Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2023; and 

3.	MDDRM Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Accountant, to regularly monitor the implementation of LDRRM PPAs and utilization of LDRRM Funds.
observations.managementAction:
Though, the LDRRM Officer prepared the LDRRMF Investment Plan, but still lacking of the two thematic areas such as disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery.




The Mun. Accountant submitted the Report on Utilization of DRRMF as required.
observations.reason: The LDRRMF Plan only included 2 thematic areas namely, preparedness and mitigation. It did not include the disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended the following courses of action:

•Prepare lapsing schedule for the depreciation of the depreciable components of the Local Road Network.

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
1. Prepare the aging of dormant receivables and identify the status or condition
of each accounts;
2. Prepare the request for write-off and the necessary supporting documents
required in COA Circular No. 2016-005;
3. Adjust the payables account after exhausting all possible remedies to identify
the account balances; and
4. Sufficiently disclose in the financial statements additional information that
may aid the readers as well as the decision makers and other end-users of the
financial statements.
1.10 During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant justified that she had already
exhausted her efforts to look into the details but there were no documents that would
substantiate the account balances. As regards to the recommendation she further said
that she will comply with the requirements and submit the request for write-off for
receivables and payables as cited in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
1.11 The audit team reiterated that the Municipal Accountant and other responsible
personnel should exhaust all efforts to look into the documents and apply all legal
remedies to collect the receivables and to dig into the legitimacy of the claims against
the LGU, and the writing off of the accounts will be the last recourse that management
should consider. The report required in the Circular should be prepared and submitted
together with the supporting documents so that their request for write off be given due
course.

2.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
 the Supply officer to conduct physical count of Inventory at least for every
semester and reconcile the results thereof with the records maintained in
the Accounting Office;
 the RHU personnel to ensure accurate reporting of inventory issuances and
item on hand;
 the division heads also to conduct inventory on all items found at his/her
office and the report on the actual status of the items found and to submit
basis a report on utilization or consumption to the Supply Officer thereof
the SSMI; and
 the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the
accounting books to ensure the fair presentation of the Inventory and other
affected accounts in the financial statements.
2.12 During the exit conference, the Municipal Health Officer said that the RHU is
conducting house to house visitation in interior barangays to conduct medical mission
or medical check-up wherein medicines are distributed to the patients/constituents and
considered dispensed at their end but there were times that not all medicines were
distributed, hence these were brought to the RHU office as inventory. However, they
did not revised the report/ recorded dispensed medicines as well as the inventory on
hand thus, resulting in the over reported inventory. On the other hand, under reported
inventory was due to dispensed medicines not recorded/adjusted in the records.
2.13 The audit team reiterated that records should be maintained to monitor the in and out
of drugs and medicines from the RHU. It should be updated and adjusted accordingly
for any movement in order to come up with accurate inventory of medicines and
reconciled with the actual inventory on hand. Also the report indicating the name of
the patient treated and the dispensed medicine should also be prepared and submitted
to facilitate verification and to determine the three months/requirement of drugs and
medicines to avoid overstocking.

3.8 We recommended the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the necessary journal entries to adjust the error made in recording the
DRR Equipment procured pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 and Item 5.1.7
of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
3.9 During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant admitted that this was
erroneously recorded to watercrafts account and that this will be adjusted accordingly.
3.10 The Audit Team reiterated that equipment purchased intended for the relief and rescue
operations should be recorded under the Disaster and Response Account segregated
from those intended for regular operations.

4.12 We recommended the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to
strictly follow accounting treatment for PPE and its related depreciation charges
and prepare the necessary journal entries to adjust the errors made pursuant
IPSAS No. 1 and 17.
4.13 The Municipal Accountant agreed to review the depreciation provided to each PPEs
and adjust accordingly the errors found in the computation of the depreciation.
4.14 The Audit Team maintained that all rules and regulations pertaining to the calculation
of depreciation of depreciable assets shall be observed in order to come up with an
approximate realizable value of the PPEs.

5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry
Voucher (JEV) to correct the affected accounts and henceforth, properly record
all obligations at gross amount as at year end and as they occur to ensure correct
amounts of unpaid obligations incurred during the year.
5.8 The Municipal Accountant noted the audit observations and will correct/adjust the
recorded amount. Likewise, they will strictly implement the cut-off on submission of
claims so that all obligations are properly recorded as accounts payable during the year.
5.9 The Municipal Accountant should see to it that all transactions that occurred during the
period be considered in the same period in order to have a complete report of all
obligations incurred for the period under audit. For monitoring purposes, the Municipal
Accountant shall prepare a report on the claims pending on her office indicating the
reasons therefor.

6.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare journal entry to book up the Cash Tickets on hand with
face value of ₱562,500.00 as of December 31, 2020.
6.7 The Municipal Treasurer noted the audit observation and will comply with the
preparation of the report.
6.8 The consolidated report of accountability for accountable forms with money value
should be prepared and submitted by the Municipal Treasurer considering the report of
the deputized collectors on a regular basis and the balances reported thereon should be
properly accounted and reported.

7.9 We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant
to strictly follow the accounting and reporting guidelines for Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) of Local Government Units
provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002, dated September 12, 2012. We further
recommend that adjusting journal entries be prepared to correct the erroneous
accounting entries made and the questionable beginning balance of Trust Liability
 Disaster Risk Reduction and Management.
7.10 The Municipal Accountant noted the deficiencies and will reclassify the recording of
transactions to reconcile the GL and financial statements balances.

8.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the year
end trial balance and financial statement for the SAGF-BGCM in compliance
with the rules and regulations set under Section 110 of Chapter 6, Volume I of the
Manual on the NGAS for LGUs for fair presentation, transparency and
accountability of the funds utilized. Likewise, subsidiary ledgers be prepared
pursuant to Section 107 of Chapter 6, Volume I, of the same manual.
8.8 During the audit exit conference, the Municipal Accountant noted the observation and
will comply the preparation and submission of separate financial statements and
subsidiary ledgers for the fund.
8.9 The financial statements for the Special Fund shall form part of the schedules that
would support the General Financial Statements pursuant to 110 of Chapter 6 of the
Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.

9.8 We recommended the Local Chief Executive provide storage room for the
Property and Supply Office and require the Inventory Committee to prepare the
Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), and to
initiate disposal of all unserviceable properties at the earliest possible time
pursuant to Section 79 of PD No. 1445, Sections 379-381 of RA No. 7160 and in
accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 92-386.
9.9 During the exit conference, the Municipal Mayor explained that she already instructed
the officials concerned to conduct inventory count of the unserviceable properties as
these were eye sore in their premises. She further stated that the inventory had already
started the physical count but was not yet finished/completed. On the other hand, the
Supply Officer assured to submit the IIRUP to comply with the recommendation.
9.10 The LGU should create an inventory and disposal and appraisal committees to undergo
the conduct of inventory taking of all the PPEs and to come up with an inventory report
segregating the serviceable from unserviceable properties.

10.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Engineer to require the contractors concerned to post the
subject warranty securities.
10.7 Henceforth, the LGU should strictly enforce compliance with the pertinent
provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR, and Item 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No.
2012-001 in the implementation of infrastructure projects, particularly on the
posting of warranty securities before final payment.
10.8 The Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant admitted the deficiency and
agreed to require warranty security from the contractors.
10.9 The Certificate of Final Acceptance shall be prepared in order to establish the reckoning
date in the posting of warranty security so that management could be free from
responsibility in case of damaged to property within the defects liability period.

11.6 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly observe Section 59c of
Local Treasury Operations Manual by devising a system which shall include the
following control measures:
a. Stamping serial numbers or the name or logo of the municipality on the cash
tickets before issuance; and
b. Affixing of the initials of the collector and date of issuance in the cash tickets
before issuance.
11.7 During the exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer showed cash tickets on hand that
were already numbered at the back of the tickets. Also, he further said that the issuance
to the collectors shall be limited for a day’s consumption only and tickets shall be dated
and initialed as control measures for re-use or re-issuance.
11.8 Aside from the control measures made by the Municipal Treasurer monitoring of the
issuance of cash tickets to the deputized collectors as well as the turnover of collections
to the Liquidating Officer shall be on a daily basis to safeguard the collections from
possible misuse.

12.19 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
1. BAC to submit the complete required procurement documents as enumerated
in COA Circular 2012-001;
2. Canvassers to require the supplier to properly filled up the data required in
the Request for Quotation and return immediately to the BAC in order to
expedite the procurement process;
3. MDRRMO and MSWDO or any responsible official submit to the audit team
the acknowledgement receipts of all procured relief goods for distribution
including the in-kind donations received and proof of receipt of beneficiaries;
4. MSWDO/MDRRMO or Supply Officer provide justification on the
deficiencies noted in the distribution of welfare goods, submit the lacking
distribution lists, completely account for and reconcile the noted differences in
the quantities of rice/grocery items procured and distributed and ensure that
welfare goods distributed are properly accounted and tallied with the record
of procurement;
5. Municipal Accountant to record the welfare goods expenses based on the
distribution list submitted and submit a complete and properly filled up
supporting documents for the procurement of other related expenses intended
for the COVID 19 relief and recovery activities; and
6. MHO/RHU personnel to submit inventory and distribution of medicines/PPEs
and other essential COVID supplies course through their office.
12.20 In the exit conference, the concerned LGU officials justified that all relief goods were
distributed to the affected families and distribution list were prepared and attached to
the Disbursement Vouchers because payment will not be made unless there was a
distribution list. The MSWDO even promised to submit the distribution list.
12.21 The audit team maintained that the distribution list they were referring to were one of
the lacking document identified during the post audit. Day after the exit conference, a
distribution list was submitted to COA, however the procured and donated relief goods
were not fully accounted for by the submitted document.

13.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LFC to strictly
observe the reportorial and posting requirement on Fund Utilization and Status
of PPA Implementation of the Bayanihan Fund Grant to Municipalities as
prescribed in LBC No. 125 dated April 13, 2020.
13.11 We further recommended that the LFC coordinate with other officials responsible
in the identification and procurement of the P/P/As to expedite the procurement
process by assessing the needs of the constituents in order to be responsive in
addressing the effects of the COVID 19.
13.12 Moreover, we recommended that Fund Utilization Report and the Status of
Implementation of the Bayanihan Grant Fund shall include local funds
augmented for the COVID 19 PPAs that would reflect the accurate balance of the
fund as of December 31, 2020, duly signed by the proper authorities.
13.13 During the exit conference, the officials disclosed that the creation of LGU website is
on-going, thus the report was not posted. Alternately, the Municipal Budget Officer
explained that the report on fund utilization and implementation of the PPAs were
submitted to DBM and to the Senate thru email while the hard copy was mailed.
13.14 The audit team recognized the explanation and justification by management that they
are compliant to the posting requirement, however, during the exit conference they
were not able to submit or show any evidence of the emails to the DBM and to the
Oversight Committee nor the hard copy of the reports. The said reports were submitted
at a later date.

14.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Supply
Officer/ in charge of the receipts and distribution to prepare the (1) Summary/List
of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for In-Kind Donations; (2)
Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief Goods; and
furnish the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit
Team within ten working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA
Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009. Henceforth, the said reports should be
prepared and submitted for in-kind donations in the future.
14.11 The MSWDO agreed to the observations and will prepare and submit the reports
required for donated goods.
14.12 The Supply Officer and the MSWDO did not prepare the Reports herein mentioned
even after the exit conference. Only the photocopy of the Report Distribution Sheet
(RDS) was submitted by the MSWDO.

15.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Social
Welfare and Development Officer and the Municipal Health Officer to:
 Ensure the proper safekeeping of the food items, and medicines and medical
supplies;
 Strictly implement the timeline on the distribution/transfer of the inventories
and monitor properly and fast track the distribution of relief goods to its
intended beneficiaries, to prevent expiration of medicines and spoilage of
food supplies and wastage of government funds;
 Formulate internal control policies necessary to minimized the occurrence
of spoiled food items such as establishing adequate control on custodianship,
issuances, and stock level monitoring through the maintenance of inventory
database to facilitate retrieval of data on inventory balances, actual
condition of stocks; and
 Prepare an inventory report of fast moving and slow moving medicines to
guide the BAC in the procurement of medicines for prepositioning in their
subsequent transactions.
15.12 The MSWDO confirmed that absence of adequate pallets to support the sacks of rice
and protect from moisture and the condition of the storage room attributed to the
spoilage of the rice.
15.13 While, the MHO justified that the medicines were supposed to be distributed to
barangays but because of the COVID 19 pandemic situation which limited the
movement of the personnel in going to the barangays, said medicines have expired at
their custody.
15.14 Considering the situation, the LGU should not purchase rice to be stored for a long
period of time due to the poor storage facility. The MSWDO and MDRRMO should
sit down and make proper planning to avoid spoilage or wastage of government funds
in the future.
15.15 Likewise, the RHU should also make an evaluation of drugs and medicines that are fast
and slow moving and avoid overstocking of medicines in order to avoid its expiration
while in their custody. Alternate activities should have been implemented to expedite
distribution of rice and drugs and medicines.

16.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the municipal officials
responsible in the procurement, canvass and other related activities to expedite on
the procurement process undertaken by the Municipality during the COVID 19
pandemic situation; and ensure that similar situation in the future be addressed
in a timely, economical and effective manner to safeguard the funds and to
optimally utilized thereof for the intended purposes.
16.10 During the exit conference, the BAC Chairman admitted that there was a delay in the
procurement process because of the limited movements due to pandemic and also the
WFH scheme. He admitted that there was really a poor planning because the data given
by the MHO as to the estimated beneficiaries in the procurement of PPEs and other
COVID 19 essential supplies were not properly calculated thus the procurement was
made as the need arises and PRs were not consolidated, they act on a piece meal basis.
16.11 The Audit Team maintained had there been proper planning in the procurement and
factors affecting the pandemic situation should have been considered, consultation with
other LGUs and technical group been made, the utilization of the fund should have
been maximized and that they are more effective in the response to the relief activities.

17.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a. The MPDO, Municipal Engineering Office and other implementing offices to
coordinate and strategize to optimally utilize the 20% DF; and regularly
monitor the status of utilization and implementation of the PPAs identified;
and
b. The Municipal Engineer/Accountant to submit the lacking Certificate of
Completion on the Construction of Day Care Center and Improvement of
Lope de Vega Signage and the justification on the questionable date of
acceptance and inspection of the constructed day care center.
17.12 The Municipal Engineer explained that majority of the PPAs under the 20%
Development Fund were implemented and completed in CY 2021. He further disclosed
that the date of the acceptance was overlooked in the preparation of documents.
Nevertheless, this will be corrected and the lacking Certificate of Completion of two
projects will be submitted.
17.13 The Audit Team maintained that documents should be prepared meticulously so as not
to overlook pertinent dates on the documents for this would have a legal implication.
The documents should be prepared on time and not hastily done and should be reviewed
before processing the payment and submission to COA and other offices.

18.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. Direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (PSWMB) to comply
with the recommendations by the DENR-EMB to facilitate the endorsement of
10-year Comprehensive Ecological Solid Waste Management (SWM) Plan
(2018-2028) to NSWMC for approval; and
2. Monitor and oversee the implementation of the Ecological Solid Waste
Management Plan and ensure optimum utilization of the fund in order to
facilitate compliance with RA No. 9003 and achieve the objectives thereof.
18.6 MENRO reasoned that some of the PPAs were not implemented during the year due to
the pandemic and health restrictions but most of these projects were implemented and
completed in CY 2021.
18.7 He further stated that the Solid Waste Management Plan was already reviewed by the
DENR Regional Office while at their end, they have already complied with the required
items which was the reason of the pending approval. The time table to submit the Plan
allegedly is on or before June 30, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Establish and book up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of
each year pursuant to RA No. 7160 and credit to the receivables account
for payments made by taxpayers at the gross amount;
b. Ensure that the reduction in the RPT and SET Receivables account for
payment of taxes were in gross amount, instead at net of discount to reflect
an accurate account balances;
c. Recognize advance payments of taxes made from prior year in the current
year applicable as revenue;
d. Discount on the advance payments of taxes should be recorded as asset
under Discount on Advance Payments account instead as Discount on RPT-
basic and RPT-SET;
e. effect the necessary adjustments on the errors to correct the reported
balances of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements.

2.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Enforce the liquidation by the implementing agencies concerned by
submitting the liquidation reports as well as the progress reports of the
projects and require refund of the unused funds, if any;
b. Send follow-up demand letters to implementing agencies – Lope de Vega
National High School and the barangays (Upper Caynaga, San
Francisco, Lower Caynaga and Getigo) for the liquidation of financial
assistance and stop the grant of additional financial assistance unless the
previous releases are liquidated;
c. Require Barangay San Miguel to refund the excess/unutilized fund; and
d. prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the affected accounts
and henceforth, properly classify transactions and record to appropriate
accounts to ensure correct balances of accounts in the financial
statements.

3.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Inventory Committee to complete the physical inventory-taking,
and conduct the same annually, in order to prepare a reliable RPCPPE and
present the accurate PPE account balances;
b. instruct the Property Custodian to indicate the date and cost of
acquisition/purchase of PPE; and
c. require the Municipal Accountant and General Service Officer to
maintain/update and reconcile the balances of their respective property
records.

4.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to include in the conduct of the physical
inventory of PPEs the local road networks and prepare the Report on the
Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN);
b. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and
the General Service Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects; and
c. Require the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to
maintain the required Local Road Network Property and Ledger Cards,
respectively.

5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. remit in full and on time within the prescribed due date all taxes withheld
in a certain month;
b. review and reconcile the balance of the Due to BIR account and to remit
any unremitted monthly taxes withheld; and
c. prepare JEV to correct the P10,480.44 representing the RPT share of the
Province which was erroneously recorded in the Due to BIR account
instead to the Due to LGU account.

6.13 We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
determine the breakdown of the account balance, trace and identify the
discrepancies, prepare adjusting entries and remit all premium contributions
due to PhilHealth pursuant to the Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations of the National Health Insurance Act of 2013.

Accountant to:

support the account balance;
b. prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the Due to NGAs and
Government Equity accounts; and
c. refund/return the excess/unutilized balance to the source agency.

8.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer
and other officials concerned to undertake tangible aggressive tax collection
efforts and develop strategies to improve their tax collection efficiency.

8.12 We further recommended to avail legal remedies for the collection of the
delinquent tax or by judicial action to enforce collection from delinquent real
property taxpayers, if warranted:
a. By administrative action through levy on real property (issuance of a
warrant on or before, or simultaneously with, the institution of the civil
action for the collection of the delinquent tax; or
b. By judicial action (filing of civil action in any court of competent
jurisdiction).

a. direct the MDRRMO to reprogram the PPAs identified as capital outlay
in CY 2019 to other disaster-related projects/activities upon submission
of a revised Annual Investment Plan as approved by the local sanggunian
and the Local Chief Executive;

in order to effectively increase the level of preparedness of the
municipality from the occurrence of natural calamities and mitigate the
risk of damage in case of disaster;

assistance received totaling P484,500.00 to the special trust fund
accounts of DRRM funds; and

projects and instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and

Henceforth, ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized in

b. Refrain from appropriating for lump-sum activities, instead observe the

a. submit the contract and all the documents forming part thereof to the
Auditor within five working days from approval of the contract; and
b. submit the abovementioned lacking documents/deficiencies. Henceforth,
carefully prepare the Bidding Documents to ensure substantial
compliance with the pertinent provisions of IRR RA No. 9184.

13.7 Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to review the completeness of the
supporting documents before payment of mobilization fee and/or progress
billings to the contractor which she certifies as to completeness of supporting
documents in the DV to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

14.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to observe
provisions of IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly:
a. posting requirements on all of its procurement. Notices of Award and
to Proceed, and approved contract be posted in the PhilGEPS, the website
of the municipality, if any, and any conspicuous place in the premises of
the Procuring Entity;
b. ensuring information on the contract agreement is complete and
properly dated;
c. the conduct of pre-procurement conference for procurement of
infrastructure projects with an approved budget of the contract of more
than P5,000,000.00; and
d. the submission of the contractor of the Construction Safety and Health
Program approved by DOLE.

1.5 We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal
Treasurer to:
1. Monitor the remittances of all collecting officers and require them to
remit all collections daily and intact or as soon as the collection reach
P10,000.00 to safeguard the government funds to possible loss or misuse;
2. Provide details on the deposited amount as to the particular
collections of the collecting officer; and
3. Refrain from remitting/reporting the prior year collections in the
current year so that collections and income shall be properly
accounted for.

2.14 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive designate an Inspection
Officer responsible for the verification of supplies delivered and to:
1. Require the Property Custodian to submit inventory report; maintain
stock cards and conduct reconciliation with the Accounting office; and
prepare and submit the SSMI supported with RIS for all issued supplies
and materials; and
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards and
attach the SSMI, RIS on the issued supplies as basis of recognizing
inventory expense.

3.14 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an inventory
committee to conduct inventory count and submit the Report of Physical
Count of PPE and instruct the Accounting and Property Office to conduct
reconciliation of the PPE ledger cards and property cards.

4.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct Accounts
Payable and Due to BIR accounts and henceforth, properly classify
transactions and record to appropriate accounts to ensure the correctness of
the balances of the accounts.

5.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget
Officer and Municipal Accountant to transfer and report the unexpended
balance of LDRRMF at year-end and transfer the unexpended balance of CY
2012 to the General Fund.

6.9 We recommended that management, through the Municipal Accountant,
monitors the net take home pay of each employee/official and makes sure that
these are equivalent to or more than the amount required in the annual
General Appropriations Act.

7.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Sangguniang Bayan in
coordination with MSDWO to formulate guidelines for the granting of
financial assistance to constituents aligned with DSWD Memorandum Circular
No. 2 as amended by DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 15, series of 2014.
Moreover, coordinate with the MSWDO to assess requests for financial
assistance by way of social case study as basis in determining the amount to be
given to the beneficiaries.

8.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MSWDO to
require clients to submit the necessary documents as basis of the
assessment/evaluation. Furthermore, direct the Municipal Accountant to
process only claims that are completely and properly supported prior to
processing / release of payment.

9.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to stamp PAID all disbursement vouchers including the supporting
documents upon release of payment to prevent the double payment and other
irregular use of the same on similar transaction.

10.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MDRRMO to
prepare the LDRRMFIP in the prescribed format and include the utilization
of the unexpended balance from prior years; and prepare/submit a duly
certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th
day after the end of each month. Further, direct the Municipal Accountant
to provide adequate disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statement.

11.10 We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board,
through the MENRO, ensure the inclusion of the budget estimate and PPAs,
as contained in the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan, to the annual
budget and Annual Investment Plan. Likewise, employ methods or strategies
with which the expected outputs provided in the plan will be attained.

12.12 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all members of the
GFPS including the elected officials and department heads undergo capacity
building programs on GAD for the effective planning and implementation of
GAD PPAs and ensure full implementation of the policies/strategies and
programs/projects outlined in the GAD Plan.

13.8 We recommended that the Local School Board observe the guidelines under
DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 in the utilization of SEF
and maximize the utilization of the fund to supplement the needs for the
operation and maintenance of public schools within the municipality.

14.11 We recommended that Municipal Development Council refrain from funding
ineligible PPAs from the 20% Development Fund and observe the guidelines
provided under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and
DBM in the preparation and utilization of funds. Likewise, ensure optimum
utilization of the fund to provide immediate benefits to the constituents and
help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental
outcomes.

We recommend that the management create an inventory committee that will focus on the conduct of the physical count of all properties of the LGU and require the Committee to prepare and submit a report thereon. Moreover, require the Property Custodian and Accountant to maintain complete records of all PPEs to facilitate the reconciliation of the physical count against records of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
and Treasurer to render their accounts and submit the required financial
reports and the financial statements within the reglementary period. Henceforth, strictly observe
Section 347 of RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the submission of
financial accounts.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to include the KALAHI CIDDS transactions in the report
preparation particularly the Bank Reconciliation Statements and the
financial statements to arrive at the accurate account balance of the Cash in
Bank- LCCA as of December 31, 2017.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor establish and appoint, as soon as
possible the Office of the Senior Citizens Affairs (OSCA) and OSCA Head,
respectively, to facilitate the implementation of the “Expanded Senior
Citizens Act of 2010,” and giving to qualified senior citizens the rights and
privileges due to them under the law.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor thru the concerned officials to
appropriate the required one (1) % from its IRA in the LGUs Annual Budget in
compliance with RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated
July 4, 2012. We further recommend that the LCPC propose and implement more
Programs/Projects/Activities that advocates child rights in the utilization of LCPC
fund, and conduct monitoring/interventions on children’s programs and projects.

We recommend that management strictly observe the guidelines on the
appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the annual internal revenue
allotment for development projects as provided for in DILG/DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. Further, we
recommend that management exert efforts to optimally utilize the
appropriations under 20% Development Fund.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to allocate at least 5% of the
estimated revenue from regular sources. Direct the Municipal LDRRM
Officer and Accountant to prepare/submit the LDRRMF Investment Plan,
Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRRM Fund and required
financial reports within the prescribed time. Further, require the Municipal
Budget Officer to strictly observe proper charging to the fund.

In order for the funds to be utilized in accordance with the purpose, we
recommend that:
a. The LSB prepare the Annual SEF Budget following the procedures
set forth in the Joint Circular cited and furnish the Local Accountant,
Local Treasurer and Local Budget a copy of the budget;
b. The Local Accountant prepare the quarterly and annual report on the
SEF Utilization using the format prescribed in the Joint Circular.
c. Observe the documentary requirements in all transactions charged to
the fund and submit the lacking requirements mentioned in the
report.

Attach/Submit the duly certified copy of the Job Order
Contracts/Service Contracts by the HRMO with statement of the
expected work or output;
Require each payee to prepare the accomplishment report duly
verified and accepted by the immediate supervisor;
Submit duly filled and signed Daily Time Record and assign time
keeper for those on the field to check on the attendance or services
rendered by the employees;
Assess its existing organizational structure so that the ideal number of
plantilla/casual as well as job order personnel will be established and
Come up with a data base of all casual and job order personnel and
maintain records on their job order personnel such as ID Picture,
address, contact number and other documents to assist in verifying
their identity.

We recommend that GAD Focal Person observe the schedule of preparation
and submission of Gender and Development Plan and Budget. Also, the GAD
Accomplishment Report should be prepared and submitted to COA for audit.

We recommend that management should workout to have an approved 10-
year solid waste management plan pursuant to the provisions of sections 16
and 17 of RA 9003. Likewise, we recommend that management strictly
enforce the other relevant provisions with emphasis on Sec. 20 of the said law
in order to attain a systematic and comprehensive solid waste management
for the protection of public health and the environment.

We recommend management:
1. Direct the Accountant to require the presentation of official receipt for
taxi fare or a certificate of expenses duly signed by the immediate
supervisor in lieu of the official receipt;
2. That granting of travelling allowance for officials and employees be
limited to the amount authorized by the Executive Order 298. Claims for
actual expenses should only be allowed under justifiable reasons certified
by the agency head to be attached to the disbursement voucher;
3. Further, direct the Accountant to cease payment of claims which are not
completely and properly documented; and
4. To require the elected employee to submit original copies of van tickets,
receipts, boarding pass, bus tickets, registration fee receipt and hotel
accommodation of P2,998.00.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to submit the lacking official receipt and require valid Official Receipts
with ATPs that conform with BIR RMC No. 52-2013 in the liquidation and/or
reimbursement of claims.

We recommend that the management transfer the unutilized DRRM funds to the Special Trust fund and completely disclose the data pertaining to DRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

the 20% development fund be strictly followed and optimally utilized for

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant transfer the unutilized

We recommend that the BAC strictly observe the procurement processes as

We recommend that management create a Solid Waste Management Board

Management should monitor closely the monthly remittances of taxes to the
Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) in order to ensure that all taxes withheld during the
month are remitted within ten (10) days after the end of each month, except for taxes
withheld in December which has to be remitted on or before January 15, as provided for
in BIR Regulation No. 2-98.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to regularly and prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements for all existing depository accounts within the reglementary period and adjust the control account and subsidiary ledgers, accordingly, as called for under Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4 of COA Circular N0. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
